evidence.quote: The results presented below were obtained with MUMPS version 5.4.0
We used the Metis [40] tool version 5.1.0... All experiments were done using 18 threads per MPI process... use 2 or 4 MPI processes
Table 1 shows the matrices coming from the SuiteSparse Matrix Collection... and industrial applications... (ElectroPhys10M, DrivAer6M...)
[8] Patrick R. Amestoy... 2001. A fully asynchronous multifrontal solver... [20] Timothy A. Davis and Yifan Hu. 2011. The university of florida sparse matrix collection.
In all experiments, the working precision is set to double (u = d)... factorization precision (uf)... may vary... letters s, d, and Q to refer to single, double, and quadruple precision
LU-IR variants with single-precision factorization generally achieve the lowest execution times... lowest memory consumption... reduce the time and memory by up to 2x
variants that achieve a forward error equivalent to that obtained with the double-precision direct solver DMUMPS (the ones using ur = d) and... order 10^-16... (the ones using ur = Q)
[18] Erin Carson and Nicholas J. Higham. 2018. Accelerating the solution of linear systems by iterative refinement in three precisions.
we set τg, the threshold used to stop GMRES, to 10^-6 in all the experiments
GMRES is used in fixed precision (ug = up)... focus on variants where up >= u
We have found GMRES-IR to usually be more expensive but also more robust, which allows it to converge on very ill-conditioned problems
[17] Erin Carson and Nicholas J. Higham. 2017. A new analysis of iterative refinement... [3] Patrick Amestoy... 2021. Five Precision GMRES-based Iterative Refinement.
we specifically use the left-looking UFSC+LUA variant described in [7]
We choose to work with the default BLR settings of MUMPS, in which case the data in the active memory is not compressed
The use of BLR with a good choice of compression threshold τb generally results in substantial reductions... largest τb value for which convergence is achieved depends on the matrix condition number
[4] Patrick R. Amestoy... 2015. Improving multifrontal methods by means of block low-rank representations. [35] Nicholas J. Higham and Theo Mary. 2021. Solving block low-rank linear systems...
when a pivot is found to be too small with respect to a prescribed threshold τs‖A‖_∞, it is replaced with τs‖A‖_∞
static pivoting is often used in combination with appropriate graph matching and matrix scaling algorithms
static pivoting slightly accelerates the factorization without excessively deteriorating the convergence... there is an optimal value of τs, which is clearly problem dependent
[42] Xiaoye S. Li and James W. Demmel. 1998. Making sparse gaussian elimination scalable by static pivoting. [25] Iain S. Duff and Stéphane Pralot. 2007. Towards stable mixed pivoting strategies...

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: for different values of the BLR compression threshold τb and a fixed value of the static pivoting perturbation τs
convergence conditions should be affected by the largest perturbations max(τs, τb) and the term ρn uf
reductions in execution time obtained by using static pivoting... are larger for the BLR factorization
[7] Patrick R. Amestoy... 2019. Performance and scalability of the block low-rank multifrontal factorization on multicore architectures.

issues.reason: Text states 'fixed value' but does not explicitly specify 10^-6 in the paragraph; value inferred from Table 5 snippet.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Specify that τs was fixed at a value (e.g., 10^-6 as per Table 5) rather than implying it is explicitly defined in the main text.

evidence.quote: Theorem 4.1 applies with ε = τb... ε = τs... ε = τb + τs
Our error analysis uses the ∞-norm
instabilities introduced by element growth and numerical approximations combine additively, rather than multiplicatively (there is no ερn term)
[18] Erin Carson and Nicholas J. Higham. 2018... [3] Patrick Amestoy... 2021. Five Precision GMRES-based Iterative Refinement.

step_scores.label: fully_supported

evidence.quote: All the experiments were run on the Olympe supercomputer... 2 Intel Skylake 6140 processors
We obtain at best on lfm_aug5M a reduction of 5.6x in time and on thmgas a reduction of 4.4x in memory
All variants are stopped when they reach a forward error on the solution equivalent to the one of DMUMPS
